person.name: Gamel de Tettesworth
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Biography
Keats-Rohan has an entry for him:

Gamel
Englishman, a king's thegn occurring in Domesday Staffordshire. Liulf of Audley (one of Gamel's Domesday holdings) accounted a large sum for Gamel's death in 1129/30.

Round wrote

Gamel was by no means an uncommon name in the 'Danish ' district of England : it was frequent, indeed, in Lincolnshire and Yorkshire. But there is no question that a single Gamel held lands in Audley, Balterley, and Talk (in Audley) as a Thegn in 1086. Who his father was, or by whom he was succeeded, we do not know. A deed which forms the sheet anchor of the Audley and Stanley pedigrees will show us Gamel's lands in the hands of a single holder a century or so, perhaps, after the Domesday Survey.

Furthermore concerning the "statement which is frequently and confidently made" that the Gamel who was recently slain by Lyulph de Audley in 1130, was the man of that name who had held Audley 44 years before, "This obviously is a mere conjecture".

Parents
Burke (1838), asserts that Gamel's parents are:

Wolfric, Lord of Leek, Aldithley & Balterley, Staffs., as well as Croxton, Etchells and other places in Chester;
and a daughter of William Poncius, Comte de Arques et Thoulouse, who was the son of Richard II, Duc de Normandie.
But Round (1910), disputes Wolfric's pedigree and essentially calls it a forgery.

Children
Burke (18138), claims that Gamel is the father of:

1.) Adam of Aldithley

2.) Lidulph, sheriff of Cheshire temp. King Stephen.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tettesworth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Betchworth (Surrey), recorded as Becheswrth (1210–12), Bettisworth (1448).

variants.name: Bedsworth
meanings: English: variant of Bettesworth, a habitational name from Betchworth in Surrey, from the Old English personal name Becci + worth ‘enclosure’.

nameMeanings.name: Gamel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Fanciful spelling of Gail , also occasionally written Gaelle .

variants.name: Gail
meanings: Shortened form of   Abigail .  It was not found as an independent given name before the middle of the 20th century; it became popular in the 1950s and 1960s, but has since fallen out of fashion.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

en.lifeSummary: When Gamel de Tettesworth was born about 1043, in Tetsworth, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Wolfric Cild Vel Uluric, was 35 and his mother, Unknown Poncinsa, was 34. He had at least 5 sons with De Mottran. He died in 1086, in Audley, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 44.

Il a eu au moins 5 fils avec De Mottran. Il est décédé en 1086, à Audley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Gamel de Tettesworth(이)가 대략 1043년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 테츠워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Wolfric Cild Vel Uluric(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Unknown Poncinsa(은)는 34세였다. 그는 De Mottran(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1086년 영국 스태퍼드실르 오들리에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.
